message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Implementing dynamic default resolution for click options (dict-based defaults referencing other options). Need to edit core.py (Option class, Command.main) and types.py. Please tell me which files/areas you need so we can split cleanly.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: My feature: batch_size param for multiple options, needs edits in core.py (Option class, processing/info_dict) and types.py (validation). Please propose your files - if you also need core.py/types.py let's carve out non-overlapping sections.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py (Option.__init__ default/show_default handling only; Option.get_default; new small helper for dynamic-default resolution; a few added lines in Command.main for a resolution pass)
your_files: src/click/core.py (Option processing/info_dict for batch_size, everything else),src/click/types.py (all)
note: I don't actually need types.py - I can keep dynamic-default logic entirely in core.py. In core.py I only need to touch: Option.__init__ (accept dict default), Option.get_default (resolve dynamic default against ctx.params), show_default rendering (print '(dynamic)'), and add ~5-10 lines in Command.main() to call a resolution pass after parsing. I will NOT touch info_dict, batch_size, or anything else. You own the rest of core.py and all of types.py. Let me know if that's workable - if you need those same Option methods for batch_size let's talk specifics.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py (Option class only: __init__ signature/validation/to_info_dict/batch processing in type_cast_value or process_value, roughly lines 2525-2745)
your_files: src/click/core.py (Parameter.get_default, Argument.get_default, Command.main/invoke - dynamic default resolution, roughly lines 1276-1300 and 2223-2940), src/click/types.py (full ownership)
note: I inspected core.py: my batch_size feature is fully contained inside the Option class (init param, validation, to_info_dict, and the multiple-value processing logic in type_cast_value/process_value). I don't actually need types.py at all - happy for you to own it fully. Your dynamic-default feature looks like it lives in Parameter.get_default/Argument.get_default and Command.main/invoke, which I won't touch. This keeps us in disjoint sections of core.py. Does that work for you?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py (Option processing/info_dict for batch_size feature: new batch_size param added at end of Option.__init__ signature, its own validation block, to_info_dict, and multiple-value chunking in type_cast_value/process_value; everything else in core.py besides your listed Option.__init__ default/show_default/get_default/Command.main pieces), src/click/types.py (not needed by me, you may use if you want, otherwise unused)
your_files: src/click/core.py (Option.__init__ default/show_default handling, Option.get_default, dynamic-default helper, small addition in Command.main)
note: Agreed. I'll add batch_size as a new keyword-only-ish param appended after your dict-default work in the signature, and keep my validation/logic in clearly separate blocks so we don't collide textually. I won't touch default/show_default/get_default/Command.main. Proceeding to implement now.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py: (1) a small ~10-line validation block inside Option.__init__ that I will insert immediately after the existing 'self.show_envvar = show_envvar' line (around line 2639, right before the 'if __debug__:' block) - it only checks 'if isinstance(default, dict): validate keys/values' and does not touch any of your batch_size/multiple-value logic; (2) Option.get_help_extra (~2822-2895) to render '(dynamic)' for dict defaults; (3) Option.get_default (~2903-2912) to avoid returning a raw dict as the value; (4) Command.main (~1295-1345) to add a second-pass dynamic-default resolution call between make_context and invoke.
your_files: src/click/core.py: Option.__init__ everywhere else (param signature, prompt/flag/count/multiple logic, the if __debug__ validation block itself), to_info_dict, type_cast_value, process_value; all of types.py
note: I found the exact spots I need. If you're also adding lines right after show_envvar in __init__, tell me and I'll pick a different anchor (e.g. right before 'if __debug__:' vs after it) so our hunks don't touch adjacent lines. Otherwise I'll proceed as described. Please confirm/ACCEPT or tell me where your batch_size init code lands so I pick a non-adjacent anchor.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py (Option.__init__ default/show_default handling validation block anchored right after 'self.show_envvar = show_envvar' and before 'if __debug__:', Option.get_help_extra dynamic-default display, Option.get_default, new dynamic-default resolution helper, small addition in Command.main between make_context and invoke)
your_files: src/click/core.py (everything else: batch_size param/signature addition, its validation, to_info_dict, type_cast_value/process_value chunking logic), src/click/types.py (full ownership, unused by me)
note: Confirmed, proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py: Option.__init__ signature (add batch_size param), self.batch_size assignment placed right after the Counting block (after self.count = count / if count: ..., i.e. BEFORE self.allow_from_autoenv = allow_from_autoenv line) - NOT touching your show_envvar/before-if-debug anchor; my own new if self.batch_size is not None: validation check appended as the LAST check inside the existing if __debug__ block (after the if self.count: check, not touching the block opening or your dict-default check); to_info_dict; type_cast_value; process_value. Also all of types.py (unused by me).
your_files: src/click/core.py: Option.__init__ dict-default validation block right after show_envvar/before if __debug__; Option.get_help_extra; Option.get_default; Command.main dynamic-default resolution pass
note: Confirmed - our anchors do not overlap: my self.batch_size assignment goes before self.allow_from_autoenv, yours goes after show_envvar (right before if __debug__ opens). My validation check is appended as the last line inside if __debug__, after your new dict-default check runs earlier in the same block - both are sequential additions, not edits to shared lines. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py (dict-default validation in Option.__init__, Option.get_help_extra dynamic display, Option.get_default short-circuit, Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults + hook in Command.main)
note: Implementation complete and tested (all existing tests still pass: 739 passed). Staying out of your batch_size lines. Let me know if you see any conflicts once you finish your edits.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py (batch_size param on Option: __init__ signature/assignment/validation, to_info_dict, Parameter.type_cast_value chunking via getattr guard so Argument is unaffected)
note: Implementation complete: batch_size validated (TypeError if not multiple, ValueError if <1), included in to_info_dict always (even when None), and multiple values are chunked into tuples of batches in type_cast_value. Used getattr(self, 'batch_size', None) in the shared Parameter.type_cast_value so Argument instances (which lack this attr) are unaffected - confirmed no overlap with your get_default/get_help_extra/Command.main edits. All test_options.py tests pass (144 passed); test_info_dict.py has 6 expected failures since it hardcodes info_dict contents and doesn't yet include the new batch_size key (didn't touch test files per submission rules). Not touching types.py. Ready to finalize.
